heading: Regulation of certain nonprescription drugs that are marketed without an approved drug application
label: 355h Regulation of certain nonprescription drugs that are marketed without an approved drug application
notes: Amendments2025—Subsec. (b)(2)(A)(iv)(III). , substituted “sponsors or requestors” for “requestors”.Pub. L. 119–37, § 6508(b)Subsec. (h). , substituted “sponsors, requestors, or organizations nominated by sponsors or requestors to represent their interests in a proceeding” for “sponsors or requestors”.Pub. L. 119–37, § 6508(a)Subsec. (r). , added subsec. (r).Pub. L. 119–37, § 6506(a)

Drugs Excluded From the Over-the-Counter Drug Review, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–136, div. A, title III, § 3853134 Stat. 454Mar. 27, 2020“(a).—In GeneralNothing in this Act [probably should be “this subtitle”, meaning subtitle F (§§ 3851–3862) of title III of div. A of , enacting this section, , and subpart 10 of part C of subchapter VII of this chapter, amending sections 352, 360fff–3, 379j–52, 379r, and 381 of this title, repealing , and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and sections 360fff–3, 360fff–6, 379j–52, and 379j–71 of this title] (or the amendments made by this Act) shall apply to any nonprescription drug (as defined in section 505G(q) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [], as added by section 3851 of this subtitle) which was excluded by the Food and Drug Administration from the Over-the-Counter Drug Review in accordance with the paragraph numbered 25 on page 9466 of volume 37 of the Federal Register, published on .Pub. L. 116–136section 360fff–8 of this titlesection 360fff–5 of this title21 U.S.C. 355h(q)May 11, 1972“(b).—Rule of ConstructionNothing in this section shall be construed to preclude or limit the applicability of any other provision of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ( et seq.).”21 U.S.C. 301

Treatment of Authority Regarding Finalization of Sunscreen Monograph, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–136, div. A, title III, § 3854(c)134 Stat. 456Mar. 27, 2020“(1)In general.—“(A).—Revision of final sunscreen orderThe Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this subsection as the ‘Secretary’) shall amend and revise the final administrative order concerning nonprescription sunscreen (referred to in this subsection as the ‘sunscreen order’) for which the content, prior to the date of enactment of this Act [], was represented by the final monograph for sunscreen drug products set forth in part 352 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ).Mar. 27, 2020May 21, 1999“(B).—Issuance of revised sunscreen order; effective dateA revised sunscreen order described in subparagraph (A) shall be—“(i)issued in accordance with the procedures described in section 505G(b)(2) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [];21 U.S.C. 355h(b)(2)“(ii)issued in proposed form not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act; and“(iii)issued by the Secretary at least 1 year prior to the effective date of the revised order.“(2).—ReportsIf a revised sunscreen order issued under paragraph (1) does not include provisions related to the effectiveness of various sun protection factor levels, and does not address all dosage forms known to the Secretary to be used in sunscreens marketed in the United States without a new drug application approved under section 505 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (), the Secretary shall submit a report to the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate on the rationale for omission of such provisions from such order, and a plan and timeline to compile any information necessary to address such provisions through such order.”21 U.S.C. 355

Annual Update to Congress on Appropriate Pediatric Indication for Certain OTC Cough and Cold Drugs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–136, div. A, title III, § 3855134 Stat. 457Mar. 27, 2020“(a).—In GeneralSubject to subsection (c), the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall, beginning not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act [], annually submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate a letter describing the progress of the Food and Drug Administration—Mar. 27, 2020“(1)in evaluating the cough and cold monograph described in subsection (b) with respect to children under age 6; and“(2)as appropriate, revising such cough and cold monograph to address such children through the order process under section 505G(b) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [], as added by section 3851 of this subtitle.21 U.S.C. 355h(b)“(b).—Cough and Cold Monograph DescribedThe cough and cold monograph described in this subsection consists of the conditions under which nonprescription drugs containing antitussive, expectorant, nasal decongestant, or antihistamine active ingredients (or combinations thereof) are generally recognized as safe and effective, as specified in part 341 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect immediately prior to the date of enactment of this Act), and included in an order deemed to be established under section 505G(b) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, as added by section 3851 of this subtitle.“(c).—Duration of AuthorityThe requirement under subsection (a) shall terminate as of the date of a letter submitted by the Secretary of Health and Human Services pursuant to such subsection in which the Secretary indicates that the Food and Drug Administration has completed its evaluation and revised, in a final order, as applicable, the cough and cold monograph as described in subsection (a)(2).”

children.heading: Nonprescription drugs marketed without an approved application
children.label: a Nonprescription drugs marketed without an approved application

children.heading: Drugs subject to a final monograph; category I drugs subject to a tentative final monograph
children.label: 1 Drugs subject to a final monograph; category I drugs subject to a tentative final monograph
i
children.text: in conformity with the requirements for nonprescription use of a final monograph issued under part 330 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (except as provided in paragraph (2)), the general requirements for nonprescription drugs, and conditions or requirements under subsections (b), (c), and (k); and

children.label: ii
children.text: except as permitted by an order issued under subsection (b) or, in the case of a minor change in the drug, in conformity with an order issued under subsection (c), in a dosage form that, immediately prior to , has been used to a material extent and for a material time under ; orMarch 27, 2020section 321(p)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: classified in category I for safety and effectiveness under a tentative final monograph that is the most recently applicable proposal or determination issued under part 330 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations;

children.label: ii
children.text: in conformity with the proposed requirements for nonprescription use of such tentative final monograph, any applicable subsequent determination by the Secretary, the general requirements for nonprescription drugs, and conditions or requirements under subsections (b), (c), and (k); and

children.label: iii
children.text: except as permitted by an order issued under subsection (b) or, in the case of a minor change in the drug, in conformity with an order issued under subsection (c), in a dosage form that, immediately prior to , has been used to a material extent and for a material time under .March 27, 2020section 321(p)(2) of this title

children.heading: Treatment of sunscreen drugs
children.label: 2 Treatment of sunscreen drugs
children.text: With respect to sunscreen drugs subject to this section, the applicable requirements in terms of conformity with a final monograph, for purposes of paragraph (1)(A)(i), shall be the requirements specified in part 352 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations, as published on , beginning on page 27687 of volume 64 of the Federal Register, except that the applicable requirements governing effectiveness and labeling shall be those specified in section 201.327 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations.May 21, 1999

children.heading: Category III drugs subject to a tentative final monograph; category I drugs subject to proposed monograph or advance notice of proposed rulemaking
children.label: 3 Category III drugs subject to a tentative final monograph; category I drugs subject to proposed monograph or advance notice of proposed rulemaking
i
children.text: classified in category III for safety or effectiveness in the preamble of a proposed rule establishing a tentative final monograph that is the most recently applicable proposal or determination for such drug issued under part 330 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations;

children.label: I
children.text: the conditions of use, including indication and dosage strength, if any, described for such category III drug in such preamble or in an applicable subsequent proposed rule;

children.label: II
children.text: the proposed requirements for drugs classified in such tentative final monograph in category I in the most recently proposed rule establishing requirements related to such tentative final monograph and in any final rule establishing requirements that are applicable to the drug; and

children.label: III
children.text: the general requirements for nonprescription drugs and conditions or requirements under subsection (b) or (k); and

children.label: iii
children.text: in a dosage form that, immediately prior to , had been used to a material extent and for a material time under ; orMarch 27, 2020section 321(p)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: classified in category I for safety and effectiveness under a proposed monograph or advance notice of proposed rulemaking that is the most recently applicable proposal or determination for such drug issued under part 330 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations;

children.label: ii
children.text: in conformity with the requirements for nonprescription use of such proposed monograph or advance notice of proposed rulemaking, any applicable subsequent determination by the Secretary, the general requirements for nonprescription drugs, and conditions or requirements under subsection (b) or (k); and

children.label: iii
children.text: in a dosage form that, immediately prior to , has been used to a material extent and for a material time under .March 27, 2020section 321(p)(2) of this title

children.heading: Category II drugs deemed new drugs
children.label: 4 Category II drugs deemed new drugs
children.text: A drug that is classified in category II for safety or effectiveness under a tentative final monograph or that is subject to a determination to be not generally recognized as safe and effective in a proposed rule that is the most recently applicable proposal issued under part 330 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations, shall be deemed to be a new drug under , misbranded under , and subject to the requirement for an approved new drug application under beginning on the day that is 180 calendar days after , unless, before such day, the Secretary determines that it is in the interest of public health to extend the period during which the drug may be marketed without such an approved new drug application.section 321(p) of this titlesection 352(ee) of this titlesection 355 of this titleMarch 27, 2020

children.heading: Drugs not GRASE deemed new drugs
children.label: 5 Drugs not GRASE deemed new drugs
children.text: A drug that the Secretary has determined not to be generally recognized as safe and effective under under a final determination issued under part 330 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations, shall be deemed to be a new drug under , misbranded under , and subject to the requirement for an approved new drug application under .section 321(p)(1) of this titlesection 321(p) of this titlesection 352(ee) of this titlesection 355 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is not subject to ; andsection 353(b)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is not described in paragraph (1), (2), (3), (4), or (5), or subsection (b)(1)(B).

children.label: i
children.text: not subject to ; andsection 353(b)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: generally recognized as safe and effective under .section 321(p)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: is determined by the Secretary to meet the conditions specified in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A);

children.label: ii
children.text: is marketed in conformity with an administrative order under this subsection;

children.label: iii
children.text: meets the general requirements for nonprescription drugs; and

children.label: iv
children.text: meets the requirements under subsections (c) and (k).

children.label: i
children.text: the evidence shows that the drug is not generally recognized as safe and effective under ; orsection 321(p)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the evidence is inadequate to show that the drug is generally recognized as safe and effective under .section 321(p)(1) of this title

children.heading: Administrative orders initiated by the Secretary
children.label: 2 Administrative orders initiated by the Secretary
i
children.text: make reasonable efforts to notify informally, not later than 2 business days before the issuance of the proposed order, the sponsors of drugs who have a listing in effect under for the drugs or combination of drugs that will be subject to the administrative order;section 360(j) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: issue a proposed administrative order by publishing it on the website of the Food and Drug Administration and include in such order the reasons for the issuance of such order; and

children.label: II
children.text: publish a notice of availability of such proposed order in the Federal Register;

children.label: iii
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (B), provide for a public comment period with respect to such proposed order of not less than 45 calendar days; and

children.label: I
children.text: issue the final administrative order, together with a detailed statement of reasons, which order shall not take effect until the time for requesting judicial review under paragraph (3)(D)(ii) has expired;

children.label: II
children.text: publish a notice of such final administrative order in the Federal Register;

children.label: III
children.text: afford sponsors or requestors of drugs that will be subject to such order the opportunity for formal dispute resolution up to the level of the Director of the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research, which initially must be requested within 45 calendar days of the issuance of the order, and, for subsequent levels of appeal, within 30 calendar days of the prior decision; and

children.label: IV
children.text: except with respect to drugs described in paragraph (3)(B), upon completion of the formal dispute resolution procedure, inform the persons which sought such dispute resolution of their right to request a hearing.

children.label: I
children.text: the general categories of data the Secretary has determined necessary to establish that the drug is generally recognized as safe and effective under ; andsection 321(p)(1) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the format for submissions by interested persons;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary shall provide for a public comment period of no less than 180 calendar days with respect to such proposed order, except when the Secretary determines, for good cause, that a shorter period is in the interest of public health; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any person who submits data in such comment period shall include a certification that the person has submitted all evidence created, obtained, or received by that person that is both within the categories of data identified in the proposed order and relevant to a determination as to whether the drug is generally recognized as safe and effective under .section 321(p)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Only a person who participated in each stage of formal dispute resolution under subclause (III) of paragraph (2)(A)(iv) of an administrative order with respect to a drug may request a hearing concerning a final administrative order issued under such paragraph with respect to such drug. If a hearing is sought, such person must submit a request for a hearing, which shall be based solely on information in the administrative record, to the Secretary not later than 30 calendar days after receiving notice of the final decision of the formal dispute resolution procedure.

children.heading: No hearing required with respect to orders relating to certain drugs
children.label: B No hearing required with respect to orders relating to certain drugs
I
children.text: that is described in subsection (a)(3)(A); and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to which no human or non-human data studies relevant to the safety or effectiveness of such drug have been submitted to the administrative record since the issuance of the most recent tentative final monograph relating to such drug.

children.heading: Human data studies and non-human data defined
children.label: ii Human data studies and non-human data defined
I
children.text: The term “human data studies” means clinical trials of safety or effectiveness (including actual use studies), pharmacokinetics studies, or bioavailability studies.

children.label: II
children.text: The term “non-human data” means data from testing other than with human subjects which provides information concerning safety or effectiveness.

children.heading: Denial of request for hearing
children.label: i Denial of request for hearing
children.text: If the Secretary determines that information submitted in a request for a hearing under subparagraph (A) with respect to a final administrative order issued under paragraph (2)(A)(iv) does not identify the existence of a genuine and substantial question of material fact, the Secretary may deny such request. In making such a determination, the Secretary may consider only information and data that are based on relevant and reliable scientific principles and methodologies.

children.heading: Single hearing for multiple related requests
children.label: ii Single hearing for multiple related requests
children.text: If more than one request for a hearing is submitted with respect to the same administrative order under subparagraph (A), the Secretary may direct that a single hearing be conducted in which all persons whose hearing requests were granted may participate.

children.label: I
children.text: be designated by the Secretary;

children.label: II
children.text: not be an employee of the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research; and

children.label: III
children.text: not have been previously involved in the development of the administrative order involved or proceedings relating to that administrative order.

children.heading: Rights of parties to hearing
children.label: iv Rights of parties to hearing
children.text: The parties to a hearing requested under subparagraph (A) shall have the right to present testimony, including testimony of expert witnesses, and to cross-examine witnesses presented by other parties. Where appropriate, the presiding officer may require that cross-examination by parties representing substantially the same interests be consolidated to promote efficiency and avoid duplication.

children.label: I
children.text: At the conclusion of a hearing requested under subparagraph (A), the presiding officer of the hearing shall issue a decision containing findings of fact and conclusions of law. The decision of the presiding officer shall be final.

children.label: II
children.text: The final decision may not take effect until the period under subparagraph (D)(ii) for submitting a request for judicial review of such decision expires.

children.heading: Judicial review of final administrative order
children.label: D Judicial review of final administrative order

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The procedures described in shall apply with respect to judicial review of final administrative orders issued under this subsection in the same manner and to the same extent as such section applies to an order described in such section except that the judicial review shall be taken by filing in an appropriate district court of the United States in lieu of the appellate courts specified in such section.section 355(h) of this title

children.heading: Period to submit a request for judicial review
children.label: ii Period to submit a request for judicial review
I
children.text: the date on which notice of such order is published;

children.label: II
children.text: the date on which a hearing with respect to such order is denied under subparagraph (B) or (C)(i);

children.label: III
children.text: the date on which a final decision is made following a hearing under subparagraph (C)(v); or

children.label: IV
children.text: if no hearing is requested, the date on which the time for requesting a hearing expires.

children.heading: Expedited procedure with respect to administrative orders initiated by the Secretary
children.label: 4 Expedited procedure with respect to administrative orders initiated by the Secretary

children.heading: Imminent hazard to the public health
children.label: A Imminent hazard to the public health
I
children.text: may issue an interim final administrative order for such drug, class of drugs, or combination of drugs under paragraph (1), together with a detailed statement of the reasons for such order;

children.label: II
children.text: shall publish in the Federal Register a notice of availability of any such order; and

children.label: III
children.text: shall provide for a public comment period of at least 45 calendar days with respect to such interim final order.

children.heading: Nondelegation
children.label: ii Nondelegation
children.text: The Secretary may not delegate the authority to issue an interim final administrative order under this subparagraph.

children.label: I
children.text: make reasonable efforts to notify informally, not later than 48 hours before the issuance of the interim final order, the sponsors of drugs who have a listing in effect under for such drug or combination of drugs;section 360(j) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: after reasonable efforts of notification, issue an interim final administrative order in accordance with paragraph (1) to require such change, together with a detailed statement of the reasons for such order;

children.label: III
children.text: publish in the Federal Register a notice of availability of such order; and

children.label: IV
children.text: provide for a public comment period of at least 45 calendar days with respect to such interim final order.

children.heading: Content of order
children.label: ii Content of order
children.text: An interim final order issued under this subparagraph with respect to the labeling of a drug may provide for new warnings and other information required for safe use of the drug.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: C Effective date
children.text: An order under subparagraph (A) or (B) shall take effect on a date specified by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: issue a final order in accordance with paragraph (1);

children.label: ii
children.text: publish a notice of availability of such final administrative order in the Federal Register; and

children.label: iii
children.text: afford sponsors of such drugs that will be subject to such an order the opportunity for formal dispute resolution up to the level of the Director of the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research, which must initially be within 45 calendar days of the issuance of the order, and for subsequent levels of appeal, within 30 calendar days of the prior decision.

children.heading: Hearings
children.label: E Hearings
children.text: A sponsor of a drug subject to a final order issued under subparagraph (D) and that participated in each stage of formal dispute resolution under clause (iii) of such subparagraph may request a hearing on such order. The provisions of subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (3), other than paragraph (3)(C)(v)(II), shall apply with respect to a hearing on such order in the same manner and to the same extent as such provisions apply with respect to a hearing on an administrative order issued under paragraph (2)(A)(iv).

children.label: I
children.text: not later than 6 months after the date on which the comment period closes under subparagraph (A) or (B), issue a final order in accordance with paragraph (1); and

children.label: II
children.text: not later than 12 months after the date on which such final order is issued, complete any hearing under subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Dispute resolution request
children.label: ii Dispute resolution request
children.text: The Secretary shall specify in an interim final order issued under subparagraph (A) or (B) such shorter periods for requesting dispute resolution under subparagraph (D)(iii) as are necessary to meet the requirements of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: G Judicial review
children.text: A final order issued pursuant to subparagraph (F) shall be subject to judicial review in accordance with paragraph (3)(D).

children.heading: Administrative order initiated at the request of a requestor
children.label: 5 Administrative order initiated at the request of a requestor
i
children.text: the Secretary shall, after receiving a request under this subparagraph, determine whether the request is sufficiently complete and formatted to permit a substantive review;

children.label: I
children.text: file the request; and

children.label: II
children.text: initiate proceedings with respect to issuing an administrative order in accordance with paragraphs (2) and (3); and

children.label: iii
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (6), if the Secretary determines that a request does not meet the requirements for filing or is not sufficiently complete and formatted to permit a substantive review, the requestor may demand that the request be filed over protest, and the Secretary shall initiate proceedings to review the request in accordance with paragraph (2)(A).

children.label: I
children.text: determining whether a drug is generally recognized as safe and effective under , exempt from , and not required to be the subject of an approved application under ; orsection 321(p)(1) of this titlesection 353(b)(1) of this titlesection 355 of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: generally recognized as safe and effective under in accordance with subsection (a)(1), (a)(2), or an order under this subsection; orsection 321(p)(1) of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: subject to subsection (a)(3), but only if such requestor initiates such request in conjunction with a request for the Secretary to determine whether such drug is generally recognized as safe and effective under , which is filed by the Secretary under subparagraph (A)(ii).section 321(p)(1) of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The Secretary is not required to complete review of a request for a change described in clause (i)(II) if the Secretary determines that there is an inadequate basis to find the drug is generally recognized as safe and effective under under paragraph (1) and issues a final order announcing that determination.section 321(p)(1) of this title

children.heading: Withdrawal
children.label: iii Withdrawal
children.text: The requestor may withdraw a request under this paragraph, according to the procedures set forth pursuant to subsection (d)(2)(B). Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, if such request is withdrawn, the Secretary may cease proceedings under this subparagraph.

children.label: I
children.text: incorporating changes described in clause (ii); and

children.label: II
children.text: subject to the limitations under clause (iv).

children.label: I
children.text: provides for a drug to contain an active ingredient (including any ester or salt of the active ingredient) not previously incorporated in a drug described in clause (iii); or

children.label: II
children.text: provides for a change in the conditions of use of a drug, for which new human data studies conducted or sponsored by the requestor (or for which the requestor has an exclusive right of reference) were essential to the issuance of such order.

children.label: I
children.text: specified in subsection (a)(1), (a)(2), or (a)(3);

children.label: II
children.text: subject to a final order issued under this section;

children.label: III
children.text: subject to a final sunscreen order (as defined in ); orsection 360fff(2)(A) of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: described in subsection (m)(1), other than drugs subject to an active enforcement action under subchapter III of this chapter.

children.label: aa
children.text: changes described in clause (ii)(I), relating to active ingredients; or

children.label: bb
children.text: changes described in clause (ii)(II), relating to conditions of use.

children.label: aa
children.text: the subject of a Tier 2 OTC monograph order request (as defined in );section 379j–71 of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: safety-related changes, as defined by the Secretary, or any other changes the Secretary considers necessary to assure safe use; or

children.label: cc
children.text: changes related to methods of testing safety or efficacy.

children.heading: New human data studies defined
children.label: v New human data studies defined
aa
children.text: a proposed or final determination that a drug described in subclause (I), (II), or (III) of clause (iii) is generally recognized as safe and effective under ; orsection 321(p)(1) of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: approval of a drug that was approved under ; andsection 355 of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: a proposed or final determination that a drug described in subclause (I), (II), or (III) of clause (iii) is generally recognized as safe and effective under ; orsection 321(p)(1) of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: approval of a drug that was approved under .section 355 of this title

children.heading: Notification of drug not available for sale
children.label: vi Notification of drug not available for sale
I
children.text: identity of the drug by established name and by proprietary name, if any;

children.label: II
children.text: strength of the drug;

children.label: III
children.text: date on which the drug will be available for sale, if known; and

children.label: IV
children.text: reason for not marketing the drug after issuance of the order.

children.heading: Information regarding safe nonprescription marketing and use as condition for filing a generally recognized as safe and effective request
children.label: 6 Information regarding safe nonprescription marketing and use as condition for filing a generally recognized as safe and effective request
i
children.text: may file such request, if the request includes information specified under subparagraph (C) with respect to safe nonprescription marketing and use of such drug; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the request fails to include information specified under subparagraph (C), shall refuse to file such request and require that nonprescription marketing of the drug be pursuant to a new drug application as described in subparagraph (D).

children.label: i
children.text: specified in subsection (a)(1), (a)(2), or (a)(3);

children.label: ii
children.text: subject to a final order under this section; or

children.label: iii
children.text: subject to a final sunscreen order (as defined in ).section 360fff(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Information demonstrating prima facie safe nonprescription marketing and use
children.label: C Information demonstrating prima facie safe nonprescription marketing and use
i
children.text: information sufficient for a prima facie demonstration that the drug subject to such request has a verifiable history of being marketed and safely used by consumers in the United States as a nonprescription drug under comparable conditions of use;

children.label: I
children.text: for such period as needed to provide reasonable assurances concerning the safe nonprescription use of the drug; and

children.label: II
children.text: during such time was subject to sufficient monitoring by a regulatory body considered acceptable by the Secretary for such monitoring purposes, including for adverse events associated with nonprescription use of the drug; or

children.label: iii
children.text: if the Secretary determines that information described in clause (i) or (ii) is not needed to provide a prima facie demonstration that the drug can be safely marketed and used as a nonprescription drug, such other information the Secretary determines is sufficient for such purposes.

children.heading: Marketing pursuant to new drug application
children.label: D Marketing pursuant to new drug application
i
children.text: the drug is marketed as a nonprescription drug, under conditions of use comparable to the conditions specified in the request, for such period as the Secretary determines appropriate (not to exceed 5 consecutive years) pursuant to an application approved under ; andsection 355 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: during such period, 1,000,000 retail packages of the drug, or an equivalent quantity as determined by the Secretary, were distributed for retail sale, as determined in such manner as the Secretary finds appropriate.

children.heading: Rule of application
children.label: E Rule of application
children.text: Except in the case of a request involving a drug described in , as in effect on , if the Secretary refuses to file a request under this paragraph, the requestor may not file such request over protest under paragraph (5)(A)(iii).section 360fff(9) of this titleJanuary 1, 2017

children.heading: Packaging
children.label: 7 Packaging
children.text: An administrative order issued under paragraph (2), (4)(A), or (5) may include requirements for the packaging of a drug to encourage use in accordance with labeling. Such requirements may include unit dose packaging, requirements for products intended for use by pediatric populations, requirements to reduce risk of harm from unsupervised ingestion, and other appropriate requirements. This paragraph does not authorize the Food and Drug Administration to require standards or testing procedures as described in part 1700 of title 16, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: Final and tentative final monographs for category I drugs deemed final administrative orders
children.label: 8 Final and tentative final monographs for category I drugs deemed final administrative orders

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A final monograph or tentative final monograph described in subparagraph (B) shall be deemed to be a final administrative order under this subsection and may be amended, revoked, or otherwise modified in accordance with the procedures of this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: establishes conditions of use for a drug described in paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (a); and

children.label: ii
children.text: represents the most recently promulgated version of such conditions, including as modified, in whole or in part, by any proposed or final rule.

children.heading: Deemed orders include harmonizing technical amendments
children.label: C Deemed orders include harmonizing technical amendments
children.text: The deemed establishment of a final administrative order under subparagraph (A) shall be construed to include any technical amendments to such order as the Secretary determines necessary to ensure that such order is appropriately harmonized, in terms of terminology or cross-references, with the applicable provisions of this chapter (and regulations thereunder) and any other orders issued under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: will not affect the safety or effectiveness of the drug; and

children.label: ii
children.text: will not materially affect the extent of absorption or other exposure to the active ingredient in comparison to a suitable reference product; and

children.label: B
children.text: the change is in conformity with the requirements of an applicable administrative order issued by the Secretary under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Access to records
children.label: A Access to records
children.text: A sponsor shall submit records requested by the Secretary relating to such a minor change under , within 15 business days of receiving such a request, or such longer period as the Secretary may provide.section 374(a)(4) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: may so inform the sponsor of the drug in writing; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the Secretary so informs the sponsor, shall provide the sponsor of the drug with a reasonable opportunity to provide additional information.

children.heading: Failure to submit sufficient information
children.label: C Failure to submit sufficient information
i
children.text: affect the safety or effectiveness of the drug; or

children.label: ii
children.text: materially affect the extent of absorption or other exposure to the active ingredient in comparison to a suitable reference product,

children.heading: Determining whether a change will affect safety or effectiveness
children.label: 3 Determining whether a change will affect safety or effectiveness

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall issue one or more administrative orders specifying requirements for determining whether a minor change made by a sponsor pursuant to this subsection will affect the safety or effectiveness of a drug or materially affect the extent of absorption or other exposure to an active ingredient in the drug in comparison to a suitable reference product, together with guidance for applying those orders to specific dosage forms.

children.heading: Standard practices
children.label: B Standard practices
children.text: The orders and guidance issued by the Secretary under subparagraph (A) shall take into account relevant public standards and standard practices for evaluating the quality of drugs, and may take into account the special needs of populations, including children.

children.heading: Confidentiality of information submitted to the Secretary
children.label: d Confidentiality of information submitted to the Secretary

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), any information, including reports of testing conducted on the drug or drugs involved, that is submitted by a requestor in connection with proceedings on an order under this section (including any minor change under subsection (c)) and is a trade secret or confidential information subject to or shall not be disclosed to the public unless the requestor consents to that disclosure.section 552(b)(4) of title 5section 1905 of title 18

children.label: i
children.text: make any information submitted by a requestor in support of a request under subsection (b)(5)(A) available to the public not later than the date on which the proposed order is issued; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make any information submitted by any other person with respect to an order requested (or initiated by the Secretary) under subsection (b), available to the public upon such submission.

children.label: i
children.text: the information pertains to pharmaceutical quality information, unless such information is necessary to establish standards under which a drug is generally recognized as safe and effective under ;section 321(p)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the information is submitted in a requestor-initiated request, but the requestor withdraws such request, in accordance with withdrawal procedures established by the Secretary, before the Secretary issues the proposed order;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Secretary requests and obtains the information under subsection (c) and such information is not submitted in relation to an order under subsection (b); or

children.label: iv
children.text: the information is of the type contained in raw datasets.

children.heading: Updates to drug listing information
children.label: e Updates to drug listing information
children.text: A sponsor who makes a change to a drug subject to this section shall submit updated drug listing information for the drug in accordance with within 30 calendar days of the date when the drug is first commercially marketed, except that a sponsor who was the order requestor with respect to an order subject to subsection (b)(5)(C) (or a licensee, assignee, or successor in interest of such requestor) shall submit updated drug listing information on or before the date when the drug is first commercially marketed.section 360(j) of this title

children.heading: Approvals undersection 355 of this title
children.label: f Approvals undersection 355 of this title
children.text: The provisions of this section shall not be construed to preclude a person from seeking or maintaining the approval of an application for a drug under sections 355(b)(1), 355(b)(2), and 355(j) of this title. A determination under this section that a drug is not subject to , is generally recognized as safe and effective under , and is not a new drug under shall constitute a finding that the drug is safe and effective that may be relied upon for purposes of an application under , so that the applicant shall be required to submit for purposes of such application only information needed to support any modification of the drug that is not covered by such determination under this section.section 353(b)(1) of this titlesection 321(p)(1) of this titlesection 321(p) of this titlesection 355(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Public availability of administrative orders
children.label: g Public availability of administrative orders

children.text: a repository of each final order and interim final order in effect, including the complete text of the order; and

children.label: A
children.text: a brief description of each such order; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary’s expectations, if resources permit, for issuance of proposed orders over a 3-year period.

children.heading: Development advice to sponsors or requestors
children.label: h Development advice to sponsors or requestors
children.text: The Secretary shall establish procedures under which sponsors, requestors, or organizations nominated by sponsors or requestors to represent their interests in a proceeding may meet with appropriate officials of the Food and Drug Administration to obtain advice on the studies and other information necessary to support submissions under this section and other matters relevant to the regulation of nonprescription drugs and the development of new nonprescription drugs under this section.

children.heading: Participation of multiple sponsors or requestors
children.label: i Participation of multiple sponsors or requestors
children.text: The Secretary shall establish procedures to facilitate efficient participation by multiple sponsors or requestors in proceedings under this section, including provision for joint meetings with multiple sponsors or requestors or with organizations nominated by sponsors or requestors to represent their interests in a proceeding.

children.heading: Electronic format
children.label: j Electronic format
children.text: All submissions under this section shall be in electronic format.

children.heading: Effect on existing regulations governing nonprescription drugs
children.label: k Effect on existing regulations governing nonprescription drugs

children.heading: Regulations of general applicability to nonprescription drugs
children.label: 1 Regulations of general applicability to nonprescription drugs
children.text: Except as provided in this subsection, nothing in this section supersedes regulations establishing general requirements for nonprescription drugs, including regulations of general applicability contained in parts 201, 250, and 330 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulations. The Secretary shall establish or modify such regulations by means of rulemaking in accordance with .section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Regulations establishing requirements for specific nonprescription drugs
children.label: 2 Regulations establishing requirements for specific nonprescription drugs
A
children.text: The provisions of section 310.545 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations, as in effect on the day before , shall be deemed to be a final order under subsection (b).March 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: subject to paragraph (1), (2), (3), or (4) of subsection (a); or

children.label: ii
children.text: otherwise subject to an order under this section.

children.heading: Withdrawal of regulations
children.label: 3 Withdrawal of regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall withdraw regulations establishing final monographs and the procedures governing the over-the-counter drug review under part 330 and other relevant parts of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the day before ), or make technical changes to such regulations to ensure conformity with appropriate terminology and cross references. Notwithstanding subchapter II of chapter 5 of title 5, any such withdrawal or technical changes shall be made without public notice and comment and shall be effective upon publication through notice in the Federal Register (or upon such date as specified in such notice).March 27, 2020
the procedures and principles for formal meetings between the Secretary and sponsors or requestors for drugs subject to this section;
the format and content of data submissions to the Secretary under this section;
the format of electronic submissions to the Secretary under this section;
consolidated proceedings for appeal and the procedures for such proceedings where appropriate; and
for minor changes in drugs, recommendations on how to comply with the requirements in orders issued under subsection (c)(3).

children.label: A
children.text: that is marketed without an application approved under as of ;section 355 of this titleMarch 27, 2020

children.label: B
children.text: that is not subject to an order issued under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: to which paragraph (1), (2), (3), (4), or (5) of subsection (a) do not apply.

children.heading: Treatment of products previously found to be subject to time and extent requirements
children.label: 2 Treatment of products previously found to be subject to time and extent requirements
A
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), a drug described in subparagraph (B) may only be lawfully marketed, without an application approved under , pursuant to an order issued under this section.section 355 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: A drug described in this subparagraph is a drug which, prior to , the Secretary determined in a proposed or final rule to be ineligible for review under the OTC drug review (as such phrase “OTC drug review” was used in section 330.14 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations, as in effect on the day before ).March 27, 2020March 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1) shall be construed to preclude or limit the applicability of any provision of this chapter other than this section.

children.label: B
children.text: Nothing in subsection (a) shall be construed to prohibit the Secretary from issuing an order under this section finding a drug to be not generally recognized as safe and effective under , as the Secretary determines appropriate.section 321(p)(1) of this title

children.heading: Investigational new drugs
children.label: n Investigational new drugs
children.text: A drug is not subject to this section if an exemption for investigational use under is in effect for such drug.section 355(i) of this title

children.heading: Inapplicability of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.label: o Inapplicability of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to collections of information made under this section.

children.heading: Inapplicability of notice and comment rulemaking and other requirements
children.label: p Inapplicability of notice and comment rulemaking and other requirements
children.text: The requirements of subsection (b) shall apply with respect to orders issued under this section instead of the requirements of subchapter II of chapter 5 of title 5.
The term “nonprescription drug” refers to a drug not subject to the requirements of .section 353(b)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is listed pursuant to ; andsection 360(j) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is or will be subject to an administrative order under this section of the Food and Drug Administration.
The term “requestor” refers to any person or group of persons marketing, manufacturing, processing, or developing a drug.

children.heading: Evidence and testing standards for active ingredients for topical administration
children.label: r Evidence and testing standards for active ingredients for topical administration

children.heading: Evidence and testing standards for active ingredients for topical administration
children.label: 1 Evidence and testing standards for active ingredients for topical administration
A
children.text: in evaluating the generally recognized as safe and effective status of active ingredients used in nonprescription drugs intended for topical administration for purposes of subsection (a), utilize standards that allow for the use of real world evidence (as defined in ), as appropriate, as part of a comprehensive evaluation of scientific evidence to demonstrate the safety and effectiveness of such active ingredients, to supplement evidence from traditional clinical trials, provided that such standards allow the Secretary to evaluate whether the benefits of such active ingredients outweigh the risks; andsection 355g(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: apply subsection (b)(6)(C) to the regulation of active ingredients used in drugs intended for topical administration.

children.heading: Non-animal testing methods for topical active ingredients
children.label: 2 Non-animal testing methods for topical active ingredients

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall consider the types of nonclinical tests described in paragraphs (1) through (4) of the first subsection (z) of (as inserted by section 3209(a)(2) of the Health Extenders, Improving Access to Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP, and Strengthening Public Health Act of 2022 (division FF of )), or any other alternative to animal testing that the Secretary determines appropriate, in the consideration of drugs intended for topical administration under this section.section 355 of this titlePublic Law 117–328

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: B Guidance
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall issue new draft guidance on how sponsors can use nonclinical testing alternatives to animal testing, as appropriate, to meet safety and efficacy standards under this section for drugs intended for topical administration.November 12, 2025

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: 3 Clarification
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to alter, supersede, or limit the standards for making determinations of whether a drug is generally recognized as safe and effective under or the standards set forth under for determining the safety and effectiveness of drugs.section 321(p) of this titlesection 355 of this title
